documentation.title: SloshingEV2D_FiniteDepth
documentation.references: Toth, F., and Kaltenbacher, M. (2015) Fully coupled linear modelling of incompressible free-surface flow, compressible air and flexible structures. Int. J. Numer. Meth. Engng, http://dx.doi.org/10.1002/nme.5194
documentation.description: The sloshing modes in a rectangular tank are computed. The tank hast a length l of 150m and a depth h of 30m. The fundamental wave number can be computed analytically by $ k = \frac{n\pi}{l} $. The natural frequencies then follow from the dispersion relation $ \omega^2 = gk tanh(kh) $, where $g$ denotes the acceleration of gravity. Analytically the first 10 modes have the natural frequencies: 1: 0.053836 Hz 2: 0.094068 Hz 3: 0.122104 Hz 4: 0.143339 Hz 5: 0.161012 Hz 6: 0.176615 Hz 7: 0.190839 Hz 8: 0.204038 Hz 9: 0.216421 Hz 10: 0.228130 Hz We numerically compute the first 10 natural frequencies (the first one is always zero, rigid body mode?). The frequency shift parameter is adapted such that the zero-frequecy-mode is not computed.
